concerns.text: The idea expects verification of *volume‑law* (thermal) scaling, but the Success Criteria only cover the clean critical case (SC‑001) and the strongly‑disordered MBL case (SC‑002). No metric or acceptance test checks that for low disorder (e.g., δ≈0.1) the entropy grows linearly with subsystem size, as required to distinguish the thermal phase.

The idea’s motivation mentions studying *boundary effects* of random perturbations, yet the specification never includes any requirement, test, or success criterion that probes or reports on boundary‑related observables (e.g., edge‑entropy, impurity‑induced deviations).
